title: Cissus oxyphylla
taxonomy: Cissus oxyphylla belongs to the family Vitaceae, commonly known as the grape family. This classification reflects the plant's botanical relationship with other species within the Vitaceae family. Cissus oxyphylla is native to certain regions in Asia, where it grows as a climber or shrub. The plant's classification as a member of the Vitaceae family suggests its close relationship with other climbing plants and vines. As a member of this family, Cissus oxyphylla is likely to exhibit characteristics similar to those of other Vitaceae species.

intro: Cissus oxyphylla, also known as the Chinese vine or bone glue vine, is a type of plant in the grape family that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The health benefits of Cissus oxyphylla include its ability to improve bone density, reduce inflammation, and aid in wound healing, making it a popular choice for individuals with osteoporosis and bone fractures. The bioactive constituents of Cissus oxyphylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cissus oxyphylla is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and tea, which are available in health food stores and online.
